channel.title: Comments for page "The Hotel California Presents: Ecce Farcio"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Hotel California Presents: Ecce Farcio"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The main formatting of this tale, and one of the reasons it's so violent, is because of the tales alluded to in the title, namely Ecce Perago and Vescatur Perago (the second being a riff off the first).

The tl;dr on those is "so much killing. so much. in really violent ways." This is intended to be a semi-parody of those.

I can't say its a complete parody because it does take itself seriously at times (rooms 7, kiiinda 6, 5, and 1), but out of all the kills only D-7294's bout of "true hyperviolence" at the very end is intended to be super serious (for a reason).

That being said, I can definitely understand the first bits being too close to the genuine article. I was more or less riding on the juxtaposition of played-straight generic edge with absurdist situations (dude fuckin kills rubber ducks and empties four guns into some dude) for them.

Anyway, sorry to hear you didn't like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe this is just me not getting it (might be a fair assumption, I didn’t really find it interesting enough to continue past room 8) but I really just see this article as edgy hyperviolence. Maybe it’s supposed to be a parody but I think it’s just a little too close to “takes itself way too seriously generic edge” for me to like it.

That’s a -1 from me, sorry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The mixture of comedic moments and horrible ultra violence is certainly a bizarre, yet enjoyable one. Your prose is as enjoyable as always, and Jr is a heckin' madman. You do good work, porpoisesnaildrinker. I fear what you will do to my critters when the time comes. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shockingly poignant yet horribly visceral: this is the moment where I go from "liking" this series to "loving" it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone who preread, including OthellotheCat, Uncle Nicolini, et al.

Fucking finally finished. God damn.
